formulas.name: Mean
formulas.notes: Calculates the average of a dataset.
formulas.variables: x_i: individual data values, n: number of observations

formulas.name: Median
formulas.notes: Calculates the middle value of a dataset when arranged in ascending order.
formulas.formula: For odd n: Median = (n+1)th term, For even n: Median = (n/2)th term + ((n/2 + 1)th term)/2

formulas.name: Mode
formulas.notes: Identifies the value that appears most frequently in the dataset.
formulas.formula: No formula, identified by the value that appears most frequently in the dataset.
formulas.variables: No specific variables, depends on the dataset

formulas.name: Range
formulas.notes: Calculates the spread of a dataset by subtracting the minimum value from the maximum value.

formulas.name: Standard Deviation (σ)
formulas.notes: Calculates the spread of a dataset by finding the square root of the average of the squared differences from the mean.
formulas.variables: x_i: individual data values, \bar{x}: mean, n: number of observations

formulas.name: Variance (σ²)
formulas.notes: Calculates the spread of a dataset by finding the average of the squared differences from the mean.
formulas.variables: x_i: individual data values, \bar{x}: mean, n: number of observations

formulas.name: Quartiles
formulas.notes: Calculates the first, second, and third quartiles of a dataset when arranged in ascending order.
formulas.formula: Q1 = (n+1)/4th term, Q2 = (n+1)/2th term, Q3 = 3*(n+1)/4th term

formulas.name: Interquartile Range (IQR)
formulas.notes: Calculates the spread of a dataset by finding the difference between the third and first quartiles.
formulas.variables: Q1: first quartile, Q3: third quartile
